title: Courseforge course_metadata.json stub
description: Canonical shape for the per-course classification stub emitted by Courseforge/scripts/generate_course.py at the course root (alongside the multi-file weekly HTML output) when a classification dict is populated. Schema mirrors the literal stub at generate_course.py:2181-2198. Phase 2 adds an optional 'blocks_summary' object that consumers can read to learn how many Block intermediates the course carries, the per-type histogram, and the canonical hash root over the union of block content hashes (re-execution drift detection). $comment: schemas/academic/course_metadata.schema.json uses MIT-OCW-style top-level keys (courseIdentification, etc.) and is a different surface — explicitly NOT this schema. The Courseforge stub uses the snake_case keys (course_code, course_title, classification, ontology_mappings) generated by generate_course.py.
$comment: schemas/academic/course_metadata.schema.json is the MIT-OCW academic-metadata surface (Draft-07, courseIdentification / topics / instructors / etc.). It is NOT this schema — they target different artifacts. Phase 2 Subtask 15 introduces this Courseforge-specific schema so the stub the multi-file generator writes has a real validation contract.

course_code.description: Course code (e.g., SAMPLE_101, WCAG_201). Mirrors the literal value emitted at generate_course.py:2182 — no pattern constraint imposed here so callers like the LibV2 course.json shape stay aligned. The stricter ^[A-Z]{2,}_?\d{3,}$ pattern is enforced at the JSON-LD courseCode field, where the canonical IMSCC ingest contract requires it.

course_title.description: Human-readable course title. Falls back to course_code at emit time when no title is in the input course_data.

classification.description: Course-level taxonomy classification (REC-TAX-01). Emitted as the literal shape generate_course.py:2184-2189 produces.

division.description: Top-level division (e.g., STEM, Humanities, Professional). Null when upstream classification dict didn't provide one.

primary_domain.description: Primary subject domain slug (e.g., 'cs', 'biology'). Null when unknown.

topics.description: Specific topic slugs/labels surfaced for downstream KG consumers.

ontology_mappings.description: External ontology cross-references. Today: ACM CCS + LCSH; future ontologies append additively without bumping this schema's $id (additionalProperties: true intentionally).

acm_ccs.description: ACM Computing Classification System concept IDs / labels. Empty array when no mapping was supplied.

lcsh.description: Library of Congress Subject Headings entries. Empty array when no mapping was supplied.

blocks_summary.description: Phase 2 (Subtask 15): optional summary of the canonical Block intermediates this course emitted. Lets LMS-side consumers learn (a) how many blocks the course carries, (b) the per-type histogram, (c) a hash root over the union of block content hashes (drift detection). 'outline_only' tags an outline-only IMSCC manifest per pre-resolved decision #3.

total_blocks.description: Total Block instances across all weeks/pages.

by_type.description: Per-block-type histogram. Keys are the 16 canonical block types (mirror of Courseforge/scripts/blocks.py::BLOCK_TYPES); values are non-negative integer counts. Zero-count types may be elided.

hash_root.description: sha256 hex of the canonical concatenation of per-block content_hash values (sorted) — re-execution drift detection. Same content -> same hash.

outline_only.description: True when this course was built in outline-only mode (no full content emitted; LMS-side pedagogical clarity). Per pre-resolved decision #3 this flag is the only signal — the IMSCC manifest type stays standard IMS CC v1.3.
